title: Carbon Fiber Production — Phase I
entry_mechanism: PSAP Track 2 — Carbon fiber JV between SABIC/Aramco and international carbon fiber manufacturer anchored by aerospace demand
executive_summary:
Carbon fiber — the ultra-high-strength, low-weight material that is the foundational input for composite structures in aerospace, defense, automotive, and wind energy — is produced by only a handful of companies globally: Toray, Hexcel, Solvay, SGL Carbon, and Teijin. Saudi Arabia currently has zero carbon fiber production despite being a world leader in the petrochemical feedstocks (acrylonitrile precursor from SABIC) that carbon fiber is made from. This is a classic Saudi value chain gap: exporting the raw material while importing the finished product.

Node AM-1 targets Phase I establishment of a Saudi carbon fiber production facility, producing T300/T700 grade (standard modulus) carbon fiber using a SABIC-supplied polyacrylonitrile (PAN) precursor. The facility serves Saudi Arabia's aerospace composites programme (MA-1, CA-2, SS-1), defense structures, and emerging automotive and wind energy demand.

The global carbon fiber market was valued at $5.8 billion in 2024 growing at 11.2% CAGR. A Saudi carbon fiber producer with SABIC's low-cost acrylonitrile supply chain would be cost-competitive globally. Full domestic demand from Saudi aerospace, defense, automotive, and renewable energy programmes alone justifies the Phase I investment.

companies.name: Toray Industries
companies.rationale: Toray is the world's largest carbon fiber producer with 35% global market share. A Toray-SABIC JV for Saudi carbon fiber production would be the most technically credible partnership.

companies.name: Hexcel Corporation
companies.rationale: Hexcel produces aerospace-grade carbon fiber and prepreg. ITAR considerations for defense grades but commercial aerospace grades are accessible.

companies.name: SGL Carbon
companies.rationale: SGL Carbon produces carbon fiber and has established JVs in multiple countries. Non-ITAR German technology with active industrial partnerships.
companies.contact: SGL Carbon VP International Joint Ventures

companies.name: Solvay
companies.rationale: Solvay produces carbon fiber and aerospace composites with active Gulf engagement through SABIC relationships.
companies.contact: Solvay Composite Materials VP International Business

companies.name: Teijin Carbon (Tenax)
companies.rationale: Teijin produces carbon fiber and has international manufacturing partnerships. Non-ITAR technology.

partners.name: SABIC / Yarmouk Petrochemical
partners.current: Acrylonitrile production; PAN polymer research; some carbon materials research
partners.upgrade: SABIC carbon fiber precursor (PAN) supply to Saudi facility; SABIC equity in carbon fiber JV; vertically integrated Saudi carbon fiber supply chain from acrylonitrile to fiber
partners.timeline: 36 months to first Saudi carbon fiber production

partners.name: Saudi Aramco / Industrial Investments
partners.current: Petrochemical value chain; some advanced materials investment
partners.upgrade: Aramco equity in carbon fiber JV; downstream value chain from crude to carbon fiber; Aramco industrial customers as anchor demand

partners.name: KACST Advanced Materials
partners.current: Carbon materials research; composites characterisation; some carbon nanotube research
partners.upgrade: GADD-KACST carbon fiber research programme; oxidation and carbonisation process optimisation; Saudi carbon fiber grade development

partners.name: KFUPM Chemical Engineering
partners.current: Polymer chemistry; process engineering; composites research
partners.upgrade: Carbon fiber process engineering programme; SABIC-KFUPM carbon fiber research partnership; 15 Saudi carbon fiber engineers
